title: Phoenix Rise
productionNotes: Open with solo Nyabinghi drums (fundeh and repeater) 4 bars, building tension. Introduce warm sub-bass on the one-drop at 0:15. Kalimba melody dancing over dub-style reverb chambers. Verse 1 stays sparse - just drums, bass, and vocal. Pre-chorus introduces steel pan harmonics. Chorus explodes with full horn section (trumpet, trombone, sax), keys skanking on the upbeat, and layered vocal harmonies. Verse 2 adds electric guitar bubble rhythm. Bridge strips to voice, handclaps, and heartbeat kick, then builds with risers into final chorus. Use tape saturation on vocals for warmth. Heavy spring reverb on snare. Mix the bass mono and thicc. Final chorus adds gospel choir backing vocals. Outro fades with Nyabinghi chanting and nature sounds.
verse1:
Mi wake up inna digital prison, yeah
Screen light blind mi third eye vision
Scroll and scroll 'til the soul start missin'
But Jah Jah whisper, say 'Come, just listen'

Dem feed we lies through the algorithm, seen?
Keep we distracted from the Most High dream
But the fire inside cannot be deleted
Ancestor voice say 'Yuh must be heated'
prechorus:
So mi burn down the illusion
No confusion, this is revolution
From the ashes, watch the transformation
Phoenix rising, new manifestation
chorus:
Phoenix rise, rise, rise from the fire
Higher and higher, we climb even higher
No more slave to the screen, we are free, we inspire
Phoenix rise, rise, Jah light never tire

Phoenix rise, rise, spread your wings now
Sing it loud, make the whole world hear the sound now
From the darkness into light, we are found now
Phoenix rise, rise, see the crown now
verse2:
Meditation 'fore the sun touch horizon
Gratitude flowing while the frequency risin'
Dem try program we fi stay in the Matrix
But we unplug and connect to the greatness

Youth dem waking up from Babylon sleep
Planting consciousness, now we're ready fi reap
No TikTok doctrine can hold down we spirit
When you speak your truth, make sure the youth can hear it
bridge:
Selah, selah, can you feel it?
(Breathe in, breathe out, let Jah heal it)
Selah, selah, we are chosen
(Rise up, rise up, chains are broken)

I-ya-bingi chant, clear the way
New world a come, this a we day
I-ya-bingi chant, hear we say
Phoenix fly high, never decay
promptForSuno: Roots reggae conscious dancehall fusion, 76 BPM, A minor, deep warm baritone male vocals with Jamaican patois, meditative intro building to triumphant anthem, Nyabinghi drums foundation, one-drop rhythm, heavy dub bass, kalimba melodies, steel pan accents, horn section stabs, bubble rhythm guitar, spiritual awakening theme, festival main-stage energy, radio-ready hook, gospel choir backing vocals in final chorus, tape-saturated vocal warmth, spring reverb on percussion, triumphant and mystical
